title: Neomammillaria galeottii
taxonomy: Neomammillaria galeottii is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas. The genus Neomammillaria is a group of small to medium-sized cacti that are characterized by their round or globular stems and short, fine spines. Neomammillaria galeottii is a member of the subfamily Cactoideae, which is the largest subfamily of the Cactaceae family and includes many species of cacti that have cylindrical or spherical stems. The classification of Neomammillaria galeottii is further divided into the tribe Cacteae, which includes a wide range of cacti species. Neomammillaria galeottii is also known to be a part of the subtribe Cactinae, which is a group of cacti that are characterized by their areoles and presence of glochids.
